description: The test case shows a Stack Overflow with a bad array index.
The test case shows a use of an allocated memory after being freed with scope complexity.
The test case shows a weakness of improper null termination with a buffer address type complexity.
The test case shows how it is easy to get a buffer overflow if a string function is misused with an address alias level code complexity.

Command injection problems are a subset of injection problem, in which the process is tricked into calling external processes of the attackers choice through the injection of control-plane data into the data plane.
This test case shows a double free in a for loop structure.
The test case shows a memory leak occurring due to memory not being released inside of a loop.
The test case exposes a null dereference.
Memory leak, the allocated memory is never freed.
The test case exposes a null dereference.
This test case shows a null pointer being dereferenced.
If we do not check the return of scanf we do not know how many parameters have been passed.
The test case shows how it is easy to get a buffer overflow if a string function is misused with a string which is in a structure.
The test case exposes a null dereference with an address alias level as code complexity.
This test case shows a block of memory freed twice.
The test case shows a use of an allocated memory after being freed.
The test case shows an improper null termination with an address alias level as code complexity.
This test case may dereference a NULL pointer.
The test case shows a use of an allocated memory after being freed with an extra allocation and free complexity.
Using the value of an unitialized variable is not safe.
The test case shows a Stack Overflow with an off-by-one array index.
The test case shows a memory leak with a pointer which is in a structure.
The test case shows a use of an allocated memory after being freed with a string which is in a structure.
The test case shows a Stack-based Buffer Overflow.
Pointer may contain garbage and pass in the check.
This test case shows an improper null termination; fread does not add the null character at the end of the string.
The test case shows a Stack Overflow with a bad loop exit condition (and start index).
The test case shows how it is easy to get a buffer overflow if a string function is misused.
A gets is never safe for untrusted input due to lack of buffer length checks.
The test case exposes an improper null termination in a string which is in a structure.
Assigns a character to a memory that has been freed.
Test of tool ability to identify use of an uninitialized variable (flawed).
The test case shows a use of an allocated memory after being freed.
The test case shows a use of an allocated memory after being freed with an address alias level code complexity.
